description: Visualization and demonstration baselines. These are descriptive artifacts; their failure is a flaw in the visualization or mapping, not in the theory.

hypotheses.plain_statement: The polar trace correctly visualizes ζ(1/2 + it) and the loaded zero positions over the displayed t-window.

expected_signature.expected_value: visual coincidence of loaded zeros with crossings of |ζ(1/2 + it)|
expected_signature.tolerance: visual / numerical alignment within rendering precision
expected_signature.pass_rule: the polar trace passes through zero at every loaded zero position

hypotheses.why_this_matters: This is the entry-point visualization. If it is wrong, every downstream interpretation of zero positions is at risk of being misread.
failure_means: the visualization or the loaded-zero overlay may be misleading
the t-window or the zero asset may be misaligned
the rendering pipeline may have a bug
failure_does_not_mean: the underlying zero data is wrong
possible_alternative_hypotheses: the rendering uses a different conformal frame than expected
the loaded-zero list is from a different precision regime
an off-by-one or offset issue in the display
intended_inference_if_passed: the visualization is faithful on the tested window
downstream readers may rely on it for orientation, not for proof
disallowed_conclusions: this is a substitute for any other experiment

hypotheses.plain_statement: Bounded-view mechanics can map sampled zero heights into the declared bounded window if transport is assumed.

expected_signature.expected_value: all sampled zero heights map into the declared bounded window
expected_signature.pass_rule: every sampled zero maps inside the declared window

hypotheses.why_this_matters: DEMO-1 illustrates how a bounded-view reduction would behave conditional on transport. It is not a proof of NC5; it is an illustration of the route's mechanics.
failure_means: the demonstration mapping or formula is flawed
the bounded-window declaration may be inconsistent with the sampled heights
possible_alternative_hypotheses: the demonstration window is too small relative to the sample range
the iteration count is insufficient for this sample
a different bounded-window declaration would succeed
intended_inference_if_passed: the bounded-view mechanic is consistent with the declared window assuming transport
this remains an illustration, not a proof
NC5 is not formally established by this demonstration
